metadata.title: Concept paper on the need of a guideline for clinical investigation of medicinal products for the treatment of chronic constipation
metadata.document_type: Concept Paper (not a finalized guideline — identifies topics for future guidance)
metadata.note: This is a concept paper adopted for consultation, not a finalized guideline. Rules extracted represent regulatory principles and identified requirements articulated in the paper. Severity ratings are capped at MEDIUM given the non-binding, planning nature of this document.

rules.rule_text: Patient populations for clinical trials in chronic constipation must be defined based on Rome III criteria for functional constipation, requiring at least two of the following: straining, lumpy or hard stools, sensation of incomplete evacuation, sensation of anorectal obstruction/blockage, manual manoeuvres to facilitate defecation (≥25% of defecations), fewer than three defecations per week, with criteria fulfilled for the last 3 months and symptom onset at least 6 months prior to diagnosis.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Rome III criteria for functional gastrointestinal disorders define functional constipation (in adults) as the presence of at least two of the following: Straining, lumpy or hard stools, sensation of incomplete evacuation, sensation of anorectal obstruction/blockage, manual manoeuvres to facilitate defecations (to be present of at least 25% of defecations), and fewer than three defecations per week. These criteria have to be fulfilled for the last 3 months with symptom onset at least 6 months prior to diagnosis.
rules.exceptions: Chronic constipation also includes organic disease (endocrine, neurogenic, drug-induced); evacuation disorders must be distinguished from constipation.

rules.rule_text: Clinical development programmes for medicinal products in chronic constipation must use patient-reported outcome measures (PROs) as the basis of efficacy evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adequate efficacy endpoints relating to the fact that – as with all functional disease – patient reported outcome measures (PROs) will form the basis of evaluation under consideration of the fact that there is still absence of a general guideline on the development of PROs in Europe.

rules.rule_text: Clinical development programmes must specify adequate safety endpoints for medications potentially influencing water, electrolyte, and acid-base balance, with special attention to vulnerable populations (children and the elderly).
rules.verbatim_quote: Adequate safety endpoints in medications potentially influencing water, electrolyte, and acid-base balance, including in special populations that are potentially more vulnerable to these effects (children and the elderly).

rules.rule_text: Clinical development programmes must address and document withdrawal and rebound effects of constipation treatments.
rules.verbatim_quote: A statement whether life-style modification as factor influencing the condition should play a role in clinical trials and the need to document withdrawal and rebound effects.

rules.rule_text: Special patient groups — including males, the elderly, and children — must receive adequate consideration in clinical development programmes, including assessment of whether separate trials are needed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adequate consideration of relevant special patient groups, such as males, the elderly, and children (including the need for separate trials).

rules.rule_text: Development programmes specifically addressing opioid-induced constipation must include documentation of exclusion of opioid withdrawal effects in the CNS.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special features of developments addressing opioid-induced constipation only (e.g. including documentation of exclusion of opioid withdrawal in the CNS).
rules.exceptions: Applies specifically to programmes targeting opioid-induced constipation subgroup.

rules.rule_text: Regulatory guidance must define which clinical development programme supports a general constipation claim versus a specific opioid-induced constipation subgroup claim for new medicinal products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Relating to the distinction between 'general' constipation and opioid induced constipation, regulatory guidance will be needed as to which development would support general or special claims (e.g. for the opioid-induced constipation subgroup) for new medicinal products.

rules.rule_text: Clinical development programmes for laxatives used in bowel cleansing must include appropriate efficacy endpoints, necessary safety documentation, and definition of possible treatment claims.
rules.verbatim_quote: The appropriate development of laxatives for bowel cleansing, including endpoints and necessary safety documentation, and possible treatment claims.

rules.rule_text: Development programmes must address the adequacy of comparators (placebo or active) for clinical trials in chronic constipation, taking into account the large number of products available on the market.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adequate comparators (placebo or active) to be used in clinical trials under consideration of the large number of products being available on the market.
